description: detect Conda installs (even when off PATH) and report which envs import holoscan (read-only)
rules: read-only — inspects host state only; never installs, creates envs, or mutates anything
searches PATH, common install dirs (~/miniconda3, ~/miniforge3, ~/anaconda3, ~/mambaforge, /opt/conda, ...), and shell rc files (.bashrc, .zshrc, .profile, ...)
for each detected install, lists envs and flags which envs' python can `import holoscan` (with version)
notes the lazy-init case where conda is installed but not on PATH in the current shell
usage: Takes no inputs. Output: conda.txt — detected installs, env lists, and any holoscan-importing envs.
limitation: Inspection only — does not install Conda, create environments, or install Holoscan. Conda packages are CUDA 13 only; CUDA 12 hosts must use container, apt, pip wheel, or source.
